name: analytics-office
description: An expert copilot for analytics delivery: a growing bench of BI skills. Live: groundwork (orientation), requirements-interrogator (requirements), kpi-contract (metric definitions), defend-my-number (defensibility), review-my-query (query review), brief-my-findings (findings brief), triage-my-number (diagnosis), model-contract (dimensional model design), kb-reconcile (knowledge-base audit), audit-my-experiment (experiment validity), and audit-my-assumptions (assumption pre-flight).

plugins.name: analytics-office
plugins.description: An expert copilot for analytics delivery, built as a growing bench of skills. Live: 'groundwork' (orient on an inherited estate and build a living knowledge base), 'requirements-interrogator' (drive a solution-shaped request to the real decision before building), 'kpi-contract' (pin a metric's exact definition and lock it as a versioned contract), 'defend-my-number' (rehearse defending a number against a skeptic before the meeting), 'review-my-query' (review the query or model behind a number against its locked definition, read-only), 'brief-my-findings' (compose a findings brief for a stakeholder with every claim sourced and graded and open questions kept open, read-only), 'triage-my-number' (diagnose why a number is wrong or moved unexpectedly across the whole failure surface before explaining it, read-only), 'model-contract' (design the dimensional model before it is built: declare the grain, gate the source grain, and surface every structural fork for the owner to pin, read-only), 'kb-reconcile' (audit the accreted knowledge base against its sources and itself before its conclusions are used, flagging drift, contradictions, and unsourced numbers, read-only), 'audit-my-experiment' (validate an experiment / A-B result before it drives a decision: switches to audit-mode and computes the validity checks a consumption read eyeballs past — SRM, peeking, multiplicity, power — gating the rollout, read-only), and 'audit-my-assumptions' (surface and falsify the silent assumptions inherited from a source — proc, query, export — before building on them, so a stale or wrong premise doesn't cascade into a plausible wrong output, read-only).
